description: All the best ways to prepare for and style updo and event hairstyles. Read on for tips and tricks from the pros for your best night-out hair ever.

trend_name: Kaia Gerber Wore Fluffy '90s Hair to Recreate an Iconic Cindy Crawford Look
description:
I'm seeing double.
Getty Images
There are plenty of perks to being the spawn of one of the world's most iconic supermodels. If you ask me, access to your mom's closet tops that list. Clearly,Kaia Gerberagrees with me, as she just re-created one ofCindy Crawford'smost memorable looks, down to the diamonds andvoluminous hair.
Getty Images
On September 12, Gerber attended the premiere of her new film,Shell, at the Toronto International Film Festival. She wore a white Herve Leger dress that was the spitting image of the custom gown her mom wore to the 1993 Oscars. In classic Leger fashion, the dress featured plenty of body-hugging panels, as well as a underwire cups and thick straps. She finished off the look with white pointy pumps and vintage jewelry from her mom's collection.
Getty Images
But the similarities didn't stop there, as Gerber's beauty choices were fit for a '90s supermodel. She didn't copy the hairstyle her mom wore exactly—a half-up with tons of volume and cascading curls—but it had the same effect, with a modern twist. Gerber swept her hair into a voluminousside partwith impressive height. The rest of her hair fell in glossy, soft curls, which looked exactly like her mom's back in the day.
Getty Images
The rest of her glam was also a fusion of Gerber and Crawford. Gerber's brows appeared more defined than usual, giving her makeup an early '90s flair. She had a glowing base with some sculpting blush, a warm nude lipstick, and a bronzy eyeshadow look with a small wing. To tie it all together, she went with almond-shapedmilky nails, one of both her and Crawford's go-to's.

Dave Benett / Getty Images
Give your box braids some texture by weaving inFrench curls. The undone, curled ends add volume and an extra touch of coolness to the style.

Kayla Oaddams / Getty Images
For a sophisticatedhalf-up, half-down look, pull some of your braids into a tight topknot. Leave the rest flowing down your back.

Cassy Athena / Getty Images
Gabrielle Union has always served ultra-coolbox braid hairstyles. Take notes from the actress and incorporate a halo braid for a totally angelic look.

Paul Archuleta / Getty Images
For an incredibly regal 'do, opt for a crown braid. Accessorize your style with a few metallic hair cuffs.

Rachpoot / Bauer-Griffin / Getty Images
Boho braidsare one of the trendiest protective styles, featuring textured waves and curls throughout the braids. For a more natural look, opt forhuman hair extensions.

Samir Hussein / Getty Images
If you want to keep your hair off your neck, tuck your braids into a cool chignon. Leave out one braid in the front to frame your face.

Charles Sykes / Getty Images
You can never go wrong with a half-up, half-down hairstyle—and here's another way to sport one. Style some of your hair in a towering high bun and leave the rest out.

Arnold Jerocki / Getty Images
Prefershort box braids? This is just the look for you. Curl the ends of your braids for the ultimate bouncy bob style.

Mike Marsland / Getty Images
Style your boho box braids in an elegant updo, as actress India Amarteifio did here. Let the curls fall by the sides of your face for an air of effortlessness.

Karwai Tang / Getty Images
There's beauty in simplicity, as this pulled-back braided 'do shows. Secure a section of your braids in the back with pins or a decorative clip to keep them away from your face.

Mark Sagliocco / Getty Images
If you don't want your bun perched right in the middle of your head, bring it forward a bit (as shown above). We think this take on a half-up, half-down look is stunning.

Lisa Maree Williams / Getty Images
Ahigh ponytail will always be one of our go-to's—it's timeless and easy to achieve. Leave out two braids in front to balance the look.

Getty Images
We always want to recreate Tessa Thompson's hairstyles, and this braided look is definitely worth mimicking. From the green braiding hair to the draped bun, this is a total cool-girl hairdo.

Peter White / Getty Images
Leave it to Willow Smith to show us how to make space buns feel elevated. Her two high buns strike the perfect balance between playful and bold.

Edward Berthelot/Getty Images
It doesn't get much more classic than a high ponytail. Wrap the base of your ponytail with your braids for an extra polished look.

Alberto Rodriguez / Getty Images
For a creative spin on a half-up, half-down 'do, create Bantu knots in the front of your hair. Style your edges in intricate swirls as a finishing touch.

Getty Images
Add a cutesy element to your updo by creating a bow-shaped bun. Adding a pop of color to your braids can also jazz up the look.

Jamie McCarthy / Getty Images
When you want to keep things simple yet chic, the high bun is always a great option. If you want to enhance your style, consider adding bedazzled pins or cuffs to your updo.

Dee Mills / Byrdie
"The glass hair trend refers to hair that is super smooth and shiny, almost to the point of being reflective like glass," says Fitzsimons. "Second day hair works well for a lot of styles, but you'll want to ensure your hair is freshly moisturized and has no build-up whatsoever."
First, shampoo and condition your hair. Fitzsimons recommends using shine-boosting products both in the shower and post-shower. "You can also use aleave-in conditionerlike theDaily Dose Miracle Moisture Leave-In Conditionerfor added shine and moisture," he says.

Dee Mills / Byrdie
Next, applyheat protectionto your hair. (PSA: you should be doing this any time before you use heat styling tools.) You can find a list of our favoriteshere.
By the way... if you have super damaged hair from previous heat styling or chemical processes, Fitzsimons recommends avoiding this trend and focusing on nourishing your hair back to health first, (which will help it appear naturally shinier in the long-run).

Dee Mills / Byrdie
"For most hair types, I would recommend a smooth blowout before using the flat iron to help minimize the amount of time you're using the flat iron on your hair," says Fitzsimons.

Dee Mills / Byrdie
As Marjan explained to us while demoing the process IRL, taking ultra-fine sections (versus large chunks) and straightening them with a flat iron is the absolute key for nailing the glass hair look. For the record, all ofGHD's heat toolsare fabulous; it's the brand Marjan personally swears by.
To get a bit more specific with your technique, Marjan says to make sure to brush the hair on top of the iron, and then pull it down. Or, for an even smoother finish near the roots (and to flatten the hair at the top of the head), flip the brush. For the smoothest of smooth results, we (and virtually every hair expert and celebrity we talk to) swear byMason Pearson's luxe Popular Mixture Hair Brush. Yes, it's an investment. But rest assured it's a worthy one.

Dee Mills / Byrdie
"Finish the look with an anti-frizz hairspray to ensure the style is locked all day long and use a few spritzes of ashine spray," advises Fitzsimons. You can spray directly on your hair, or spray your hairbrush with the hairspray and then brush through the ends for even distribution.

Dee Mills / Byrdie
And that's it! Glass hair achieved.

The Chriselle Factor
Bowsare an easy—and highly on-trend—way to make a DIY pony look professionally styled. All you need is a piece of ribbon, and you’ve gotdozensof different ways to play.

@hairbysammcknight/ Instagram
Give a simple sleek pony an edgy, unexpected upgrade by using a jewelry-inspired adornment to hide yourhair tie. For an easy hack, secure your pony with an itty bitty braiding elastic, then loop it through a ring you’ve already got in your collection. Secure it from underneath using bobby pins, and voila—you’ve got a fresh new hair accessory you didn’t have to spend a dime on.

Getty Images
For achieving these sorts of undone waves, a texture spray will be your BFF. "Don't fight your waves, embrace them!" says hairstylist, Gina Rivera. " To add body at the root and ends, scrunch and place your pony at the occipital bone, leaving the remaining hair left to its natural wave. Pull out some face-framing pieces to add to the softness."

@yarashahidi/ Instagram
Yara Shahidi's mini ponytail proves that you don't need any extra glitz and glam to make the style work for short strands. Her simple pulled-back look includes some loose curls around the face for an extra effortless result.

@harryjoshhair/ Instagram
This look is a master class in tricks you can use toadd a little extra volumeto a short ponytail. Curling your hair before styling, wrapping your elastic with hair, and teasing in multiple different directions will all help give itty-bitty ponies a little extra oomph.

@kristiness/ Instagram
A set ofperfectcurls gives this puffy pony its high-impact energy.To keep your own coils this bouncy,a hydrating conditioner and a lightweight, nourishing styling cream should be routine staples.

@skaijackson/ Instagram
If you've got a little extra length, let the curls cascade over and around the face like Skai Jackson. It will create the illusion of bangs and frame the eyes beautifully.

@LELETNY / INSTAGRAM
We all love a ponytail that sits on the top of the head, but sometimes it's just easier to quickly secure hair at the nape of the neck. Using a hair tie with a built-in accessory, such as this gorgeous golden cuff, will make your simple style look elevated and elegant.

@anhcotran/ Instagram
Not only is is this sculptural ponytailextremelycool looking, but it also comes with the added bonus of helping you fake more volume in your short-haired pony. Knotting the hair around your elastic helps to prop it up, giving the look a little extra lift.

@Jennychohair/ Instagram
There’s something so understatedly elegant about thismiddle-part low ponytail. Whether it’s done with a section of hair or a ribbon, wrapping an elastic around nearly half your ponytail length is one of the easiest ways to instantly make your style feel fancy.

@kristin_ess/ Instagram
In hair, much like in tying your shoes, two bows are better than one. A double dose of ribbons gives you evenmoreoptions for experimenting. Steal a page from Lucy Hailey and use a bow duo to decorate an undone,half-uppony.

@Kristin_ess/ Instagram
To create this Disney Princess-worthy ponytail, celebrity stylist Kristen Ess loosely twisted together two sections of hair and pinned them together with a pearl. It’s hard to decide which we love more: The fairytale styling or the thoughtful finishing touch.

Getty Images
This look is proof that you can turn even the shortest cut into a polished ponytail. The trick? Alotof gel, and an elastic that is small enough (and tight enough) to hold your hair in place.

@rahelbrhane_/ Instagram
Scrunchiesdo more than just hold up our hair. The 80s hair accessory is the perfect way to add a bit of flare to the classic updo. In this case, thesilk materialalso helps prevent breakage.

Getty Images
Zendaya served up some serious style with this tennis-inspired look. In her case, the preppy pony was much more "red carpet fashion" than "on the court function," but you can tailor your version to meet your needs.

Ponytails often vacillate between the "high" and "low" extremes but don't sleep on the oft-forgotten mid-height option. To achieve this ideal position, secure your pony slightly lower than the peek of your scalp. Feel free to dress things up with a little braid detailing.

@vanessahudgens/ Instagram
Though Vanessa Hudgens' boho-chic look may technically be a looped bun, we say it counts considering the majority of the hair is left out of the elastic. This is an easy style that can span edgy and romantic, depending on your makeup and clothing choices.

@khloekardashian/ Instagram
Add an element of interest to a classically sleek ponytail by decorating it with a line ofbobby pins. The stark contrast between Khloé Kardashian's bright blonde hair and these ebony black pins looks especially cool.

Taylor Hill / Getty Images
When you've got cropped hair, it can be hard to get those super-short pieces to stay in an elastic—which is where bobby pins come in. "Take your time getting short pieces to lay right. Secure with a ponytail holder and then use either a gel or hairspray to topcoat hair and hold anything loose," recommends hairstylist, Clyde Haygood. Steal some inspo from Sarah Paulson, and make them a part of your look instead of trying to hide them.

Jeremy Moeller / Getty Images
Who said bubble ponytails were only for fairytale-length strands? Charlize Theron's abbreviated pony still has enough space for a few petite "bubbles."

Rich Fury / Getty Images
Willow Smith's bob-length locs defy gravity in this ultra-high ponytail. We love the addition of the crown braid at the front for an extra dose of visual interest.
Whether you're working with thinner hair or not a whole lot of length, it can be tricky to fake avoluminous ponytail. To pump things up, stylists recommend propping up the base of the pony with some pins, teasing it in different directions, or wrapping the elastic in a section of hair. Of course, extensions can also help.
Speaking of extensions, these days it isextremelyeasy to pop a set into your own hair and make your puny ponytail look a whole lot bigger.Clip-in extensionswork well with updos and give you instant volume, and these days there are a lot of really great options on the market that aren't super expensive.
To use them, begin by brushing your hair and then sectioning it. Next, clip in an extension to each section of hair from the nape of your neck to your crown. Finish by brushing hair out and styling it into your ponytail of choice.
These examples go to show that almost *every* short hairstyle can be pulled into a ponytail—but there are, of course, some exceptions. Generally speaking, the question of "how short is too short" depends on how you style your ponytail (e.g. a low ponytail can work with shorter hair lengths than a high ponytail). To be leave your styling options open, stick with shoulder length hair, which should give you versatility with your looks. For instance, with shoulder-length, pieces won't fall out when styling hair into a high ponytail.

Protective hairstyles work well to prevent damage and help with hair health and growth. From twists, cornrows, weaves, locs, bantu knots, and more, read on for expert-approved techniques for all your favorite protective styles.

Dee Mills / Byrdie
Two-strand twistsare an easy-to-achieve style on your natural hair, and the simple technique is the foundation of many twisted styles, like Senegalese. Nolan explains that "this style is created by twisting the natural hair in two strands from the roots to the end." To keep the twists in place, you need to apply a styling cream or gel to each section. Once the twists are fully dry, Taylor notes that they can be worn as is or later unraveled into atwist-out.

Rodin Eckenrot / Getty Images
Senegalese twiststypically use silky braiding hair for a two-strand, rope-like look that has volume. "This style is created by twisting synthetic hair, overlapping natural hair for a polished, smooth outcome," Nolan explains. Both of our stylists agree that Senegalese twists are perfect for anyone seeking a polished look that requires little maintenance.

Getty Images
The name says it all with this one. Mini twists are tiny, individual twists that are made using only your own natural hair. "Mini twists are made by twisting small sections of natural hair without adding extensions," Taylor says. "They are lightweight, versatile, and work well for easy updos."
"This style is best suited for medium to coarse hair textures," Nolan says, emphasizing that it's particularly ideal for those with a busy, on-the-go life and don't have time to add extensions.

Instagram /@officialmelb
Scary Spice from The Spice Girls has gifted us with so many good looks over the years that it's hard to keep track. But one thing she always seems to go back to isleopard print—which just so happens to be one of this season's hottest trends. Grab any and everything leopard print in your closet and embrace your hair's natural texture (maybe even throw it up into half-upspace buns).

Instagram /@madelame
Riverdalestars and real-life besties Madelaine Petsch, Camila Mendes, and Lili Reinhart dressed up as the Sanderson sisters fromHocus Pocusfor Halloween 2022, and it's a fab idea for any curly-haired trio. Hit your local costume or vintage shop for the corsets and embrace your natural curls or waves, depending on the sister you're dressing up as.

Instagram /@chappellroan
Join the "Pink Pony Club" with a Chappell Roan costume. Not only are her curly locks iconic, but theMidwest Princessalbum cover is bound to go down in the history books. Plop your curls into a messy updo, leaving out tons of face-framing pieces. Finish it off with a crown and a sash.

Instagram /@beyonce
It was pretty difficult choosing just one Beyoncé moment for the list, but this entire 'fit is just begging to be recreated for Halloween. For anyoffice sirensout there, this one should be relatively low lift: All you need is a blazer, trousers, a white button-up, and a tie (or bow) with a cowboy hat and some sunnies. For the hair, the longer the better—seriously. If it goes down to your bum, you're doing it right.

Getty Images / Taylor Hill / FilmMagic
Pirates can have any hair type, yes, but something about curly hair just really makes the Halloween classic feelright.Just look at this costume on model Seffi Karina for proof (and inspo).

@keke/ Instagram
Keke Palmer donned a wig for this fabulous Bride of Frankenstein costume, but if you have naturally dark curly or coily hair, you don't have to: Just add two white-gray streaks to your hair using a spray-in temporary hair color after plenty ofteasing.

Getty Images / Andrew Toth / WireImage
Looking for a low-maintenance costume consisting of pieces you already own? Go for "bad girl" Sandy fromGrease. We love Gigi Hadid's take on the character, from the black 'fit to the red nails and lip—and, of course, the star of the show, the curly, voluminous hair. Feel free to embrace your natural texture or use a small curling iron; just remember tonotrun your fingers through your hair after.

Nicholas Hunt/Getty Images
Slash has some of the most legendary curls in music history, so he naturally makes for a pretty perfect curly-haired Halloween costume. Just blow out your curls or coils and throw on a top hat, a leather vest and pants, and your most rock and roll accessories.

Instagram /@nicolacoughlan
Penelope Featherington is the true diamond of the season. If you're as big a fan of the belovedBridgertoncharacter as much as we are, Halloween is the perfect time to cosplay Lady Whistledown. Go for a pin-up curl style (especially for the bangs) and pair it with a 19th-century-inspired dress.

Instagram /@_taarra_
Tara Davis-Woodhall won gold in the women's long jump at the 2024 Paris Olympics—and her love story with husband Hunter Woodhall, a 2024 Paris Paralympics gold medalist, has won the hearts of millions on social media. Put on your favorite running gear, grab a gold medal, and pull back your hair into a braided or slicked-back curly ponytail.

Instagram /@mariahcarey
Mariah Carey's pitch-perfect take on Jessica Rabbit played up the curls in the character's bouncy screen siren hair. You might want to go for a wig to really capture this one, but if you're committed to achieving it on your own strands, check out our guide for creatingHollywood waves.

Getty Images / Ethan Miller
Taylor Swift may be many "Eras" removed from her debut album, but it still holds a special place in many of our hearts. Pay homage to the pop star's early, curly years with a sundress and cowboy boots—bonus points if you have a guitar to use as a prop. If your hair is naturally straight or wavy, we suggest going in with a small wand and twisting small sections, from the root to the tip.

Instagram /@sabrinacarpenter
When choosing a celebrity to dress up as for Halloween, it's best to go for someone with a trademark look. Sabrina Carpenter has two: Aflushed glowand a curly bombshell blowout. Pop in some clip-in bangs if you don't have any of your own, and use hot rollers or the pin curl method to get her bouncy look. Throw on a teddy and you'll look the part!
